entities.name: PEANUT
entities.description: The main agricultural crop studied for disease resistance.

entities.name: LATE LEAF SPOT
entities.description: A major foliar disease of peanut causing yield loss and quality degradation.

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The agricultural productivity of the peanut crop.

entities.name: KERNEL QUALITY
entities.description: The quality of the peanut seed/kernel.

entities.name: FORAGE QUALITY
entities.description: The quality of the peanut plant used as animal feed.

entities.name: WILD ARACHIS SPECIES
entities.description: Wild relatives of peanut that possess higher resistance to late leaf spot.

entities.name: CULTIVATED PEANUT
entities.description: Domesticated peanut varieties used in agriculture.

entities.name: PLOIDY LEVEL
entities.description: The number of sets of chromosomes in a cell, which differs between wild and cultivated species.

entities.name: IPADUR
entities.description: A synthetic amphidiploid of wild peanuts with high resistance to late leaf spot.

entities.name: TIFRUNNER
entities.description: A specific cultivated peanut variety used in the crossing study.

entities.name: TI POPULATION
entities.description: A population derived from crossing Ipadur and Tifrunner.

entities.name: RECOMBINANT INBRED LINES
entities.description: Lines produced by repeated selfing of the TI population for genetic analysis.

entities.name: WHOLE-GENOME RESEQUENCING
entities.description: A technique used to sequence the entire genome of the recombinant inbred lines.

entities.name: BIN-BASED GENETIC LINKAGE MAP
entities.description: A high-density genetic map constructed using bin markers to identify genomic regions.

entities.name: BIN MARKERS
entities.description: Markers used to construct the high-density genetic linkage map.

entities.name: RECOMBINATION LANDSCAPE
entities.description: The distribution and pattern of genetic recombination across cultivated and wild species.

entities.name: PHENOTYPING
entities.description: The process of measuring the physical traits of the plants across different environments.

entities.name: LLS DISEASE RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of the peanut plant to resist late leaf spot infection.

entities.name: QTL
entities.description: Quantitative Trait Loci associated with LLS disease resistance.

entities.name: QLLS14.1
entities.description: A major QTL for LLS resistance located on chromosome 14.

entities.name: JIYANG
entities.description: A location where phenotyping data was collected in 2021.

entities.name: HENAN
entities.description: A location where phenotyping data was collected in 2022.

entities.name: CHROMOSOME 14
entities.description: The specific chromosome where the major QTL qLLS14.1 is located.

entities.name: 530-KB CHROMOSOME SEGMENT
entities.description: A specific genomic region derived from Ipadur that contributes to LLS resistance.

entities.name: DISEASE RESISTANCE PROTEINS
entities.description: Proteins located within the 530-kb chromosome segment that may provide resistance.

entities.name: ALLELIC VARIATION ANALYSIS
entities.description: Analysis used to identify sequence differences between Tifrunner and Ipadur.

entities.name: PYRAMIDING
entities.description: A breeding strategy to combine multiple resistance loci into a single cultivar.

relationships.description: crossed with to create TI population
